blocks.content: At some point Maya went with Nathan to find a bathroom. Her mother had closed her eyes. The waiting room was almost quiet.
Thomas Hailey looked at the floor for a long moment.
Then he looked at Ethan.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: She used to get angry when we worried about her.
His voice was measured. Careful. A man holding something he'd been carrying for a very long time.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: She'd say we were treating her like she was already gone. That she deserved to be treated like someone who was still here.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: She still hates that.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: I know. She's right to hate it.
A pause. The fluorescent light hummed above them.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: I've been thinking about the card we left at the cabin.
Ethan looked up.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: I've been wondering if it was too much weight to put on a young man. Whether I asked too much of you in writing it.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You didn't.
He said it without hesitation.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: I know.
A long silence.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: Tonight. Before it happened. I was watching you from across that table.
Ethan waited.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: You were watching her. The way you always do — like you're keeping an account of something. Like you're holding onto every minute of it.
Thomas looked at his own hands. His jaw was tight.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: I have been afraid for my daughter since she was in high school. There has not been a single day since that I haven't been carrying that. Not one day.
Ethan was very still.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: And then you walked into her life. And I watched the way you looked at her. And I thought — she is loved. Whatever comes next — whatever the next year or five years or twenty years brings — she is completely loved.
Ethan's throat moved.

blocks.speaker: Thomas
blocks.content: I need you to know that I see it. That whatever happens tonight — she has been loved completely. Because of you.
Ethan couldn't speak.
He pressed his lips together. Nodded. The smallest nod.
Thomas Hailey put his hand on his son-in-law's arm and left it there.
And the two men who loved her most sat in a beige waiting room under fluorescent light and didn't say another word.
Because there was nothing else to say.
